reference: Delić et al., Science 367, 892 (2020); Neumeier et al., PNAS 121 (2024)
purpose: Calibrate TEGR FDTD engine to reproduce Vienna lab quantum ground-state parameters

particle.note: Range 70-140 nm; 10^8 to 10^9 amu. Using 100nm / 10^9 amu as canonical

trap.note: 300 kHz COM oscillation frequency; ground state ~4 pm spatial width

vacuum.note: Need UHV 10^-10 mbar to suppress thermal decoherence for macroscopic interference

interference_target.note: After 12ms free-fall, wavepacket expands to ~12nm spatial superposition

electron_baseline.note: Standard Preset 43 electron. This MUST produce clean bands.

mass_scaling.note: m_nanosphere / m_electron ≈ 1.84 × 10^15. Cannot plug directly — must use dimensionless scaling.

dimensionless_strategy.approach: Hold C and wave_speed fixed, sweep m0 logarithmically
dimensionless_strategy.dt_scaling: dt = dt_base / sqrt(m0 / m0_electron) to keep CFL stable
dimensionless_strategy.total_ticks_scaling: total_ticks = base_ticks * sqrt(m0 / m0_electron) to maintain physical time
dimensionless_strategy.note: Log sweep lets us find the exact m0 where interference transitions to classical

confining_force.omega_trap: 2*pi*300e3 in physical units → mapped to engine via F_conf amplitude
confining_force.note: Tune confining_force in engine to match 4pm ground-state width at each m0

decoherence_metrics.spatial_spread: Standard deviation σ of screen hit distribution
decoherence_metrics.slit_to_blob_distance: Distance from slit wall to first measurable intensity (Jeff's metric)
decoherence_metrics.phase_coherence: Kuramoto order parameter r = |<e^{iθ}>| across all defects
decoherence_metrics.sindy_R2: Goodness of fit — when R² drops, the RAE can no longer describe the dynamics

penrose_comparison.diosi_penrose_formula: τ_DP = ℏ / E_G where E_G = gravitational self-energy
penrose_comparison.tegr_formula: τ_TEGR = threshold where ∇γ phase-shearing exceeds Kuramoto bounding κsin(θ)
penrose_comparison.divergence_test: If τ_TEGR ≠ τ_DP at same mass, TEGR makes a distinct testable prediction
